title: What Happens to Your Paper and Cardboard After a House Clearance?
excerpt: Ever wondered what happens to all that paper and cardboard after a house clearance? Discover how Lanora House responsibly recycles tons of materials across Cornwall and Devon, turning waste into resources through our sustainable clearance process.
content:
When we do house clearances around Cornwall and Devon, especially for families moving out or clearing properties after a bereavement, we come across tons of paper and cardboard waste — from old documents to moving boxes, newspapers, packaging, and books.

But have you ever wondered what actually happens to all that material once we've collected it?

At Lanora House Clearance, we don't just chuck it all in a skip; we make sure it's dealt with properly.

## 📦 Step 1: Expert Sorting & Categorization

All paper and cardboard are not the same — and recycling them efficiently starts with proper sorting. Our trained team categorizes materials into:

• **Corrugated Cardboard** (boxes and packaging)
• **Mixed Paper** (junk mail, books, magazines)
• **Office Paper** (white sheets, printer paper)
• **Newspapers & Newsprint**
• **Confidential Paper** (securely shredded)

When we clear a property, our team sorts these at source wherever possible. Mixed materials are separated from food-contaminated cardboard (which usually cannot be recycled) and grouped by type to maintain high recycling value.

## 🧊 Step 2: Moisture Protection (Critical!)

Moisture is the enemy of paper recycling. That's why all recyclable materials are:

• **Stored in dry, covered units**
• **Bailed if necessary** to prepare for transport
• **Kept separate** from general or wet waste

This preserves the integrity of the fibres, making sure they can be fully recycled without being rejected by recycling facilities.

## 🏭 Step 3: Off to Local Recycling Partners

Once sorted and stored, your old papers and boxes head off to licensed recycling partners in Cornwall and the South West.

Here's what happens next:

1. **Shredding** – the paper is shredded into small pieces
2. **Pulping** – mixed with water and turned into a slurry
3. **Screening & De-inking** – contaminants like glue, plastic and ink are removed
4. **Rolling into Sheets** – clean pulp is pressed, dried and turned into new paper or cardboard

In fact, **70% of the paper used in the UK is recycled**, and it takes **70% less energy** to make recycled paper than to create it from raw materials.

## 🌍 Why This Matters for Cornwall & Devon

Our region generates thousands of tonnes of paper and cardboard waste annually — especially during house moves, bereavement clearances, and rental turnovers.

Without proper handling, this ends up in landfill or contaminates recycling streams.

At Lanora House Clearance, our commitment goes beyond removal. We:

✅ Sort all recyclable paper and cardboard
✅ Partner with Cornwall-based recycling facilities
✅ Prevent recyclables reaching landfill
✅ Educate clients on recycling best practices

And because we handle everything for you, there's no stress — just the peace of mind that your clearance is done ethically, locally, and sustainably.

## 🏡 Serving Cornwall & Devon with Pride

Whether you're clearing an office in Plymouth, a flat in Falmouth, or a family home in St Austell, we ensure all recyclable materials are handled responsibly.

It's just one of the ways Lanora House is helping reduce waste and protect the stunning environment we all share here in the South West.

## 📞 Ready for a Responsible Clearance?

Get in touch with Lanora House today — your trusted local clearance team serving Cornwall and Devon.

**Let's clear your space — and care for our planet at the same time.**

authorBio: Environmental Coordinator at Lanora House with over 8 years of experience in sustainable waste management across Cornwall and Devon. Passionate about turning waste into resources and educating our community on responsible recycling practices.
